Richmond recruiting boss Rhy Gieschen has confirmed the Tigers (currently second-last) are willing to bid on Carlton father-son Cody Walker and Port Adelaide Academy prospect Dougie Cochrane to secure a second-round compensation pick. Richmond is also targeting 205cm ruck Harry van Hattum, who could be a long-term Rookie Draft watch for Fantasy ruck stocks. No current player prices, scores, break-evens or injuries are mentioned.

news.headline: Blueprint or blip? What the loss to Geelong told us about the Swans
news.body: Geelong's pressure and midfield dominance in round 11 gave Sydney a reality check.
news.ai_summary: James Jordon (Sydney) shapes as a tagging option after restricting Geelong's Bailey Smith to just 8 disposals in the first half before Dean Cox controversially released the tag — Smith then exploded for 18 disposals, 3 clearances and a goal in the third quarter alone, highlighting Smith's ceiling when unattended. Sydney's midfield was smashed -25 in contested possessions at half-time with a -5 centre clearance differential, a concern for owners of Chad Warner, Isaac Heeney and Brodie Grundy. The Swans' turnover game also collapsed (-32 differential vs a league-best +18.7 average), denting scoring upside for high-priced Swans across the board. Lawson Humphries showed intercept appeal as a cheap defender watch.

news.headline: Bolt from the Blues: Carlton snatches thriller over Cats
news.body: Carlton has defeated Geelong by four points in a rollicking contest at the MCG.
news.ai_summary: Patrick Cripps sealed Carlton's 88-84 thriller over Geelong with a late mark and goal, continuing strong form for SuperCoach owners. Geelong's Lawson Humphries was helped off in the fourth quarter with an awkward landing — monitor his status. Sam Walsh was prolific before being hurt in a Bailey Smith tackle, leaving the ground holding his right shoulder; a key injury watch for owners. Carlton youngsters Jagga Smith, Flynn Young (two goals), Talor Byrne, Jack Ison, Harry Dean and Billy Wilson all impressed in expanded roles under Josh Fraser, while Jeremy Cameron (4 goals) and Ollie Dempsey remain in strong form for the Cats.

Fremantle welcomes back skipper Alex Pearce (knee), but fantasy-relevant mids Hayden Young (concussion, third week out) and Caleb Serong (calf) remain sidelined — a major hit to Serong owners. The Lions' recent form slump (6-5) and back-to-back heavy losses could pressure scoring output from key Brisbane mids, while Fremantle rolls in on a club-record 10-game winning streak.

news.headline: Bombers assistant, club psychologist quit in wake of Scott sacking
news.body: A week of upheaval has continued at Essendon ahead of the Bombers' clash with West Coast.
news.ai_summary: Essendon's coaching upheaval continues with midfield assistant Ben Jacobs and club psychologist Ben Robbins departing Friday, days after senior coach Brad Scott was sacked. Brent Stanton takes over midfield assistant duties for Sunday's clash with West Coast at Optus Stadium — a role shift fantasy coaches should monitor for any Bombers midfield rotation/role changes. Scott exited after winning just one of his last 24 games, including a 17-game losing streak, with Ken Hinkley, Adam Simpson and James Hird floated as successors. No direct player price or break-even implications, but expect role volatility for Essendon mids until a new senior coach is appointed.

news.headline: Forward to miss months in a Giants double blow
news.body: Surgery has sidelined Jake Riccardi, the forward joining Jack Buckley in the medical room.
news.ai_summary: Avoid/downgrade Jake Riccardi (GWS), who will miss at least two months after ankle syndesmosis surgery — a clear sell for fantasy coaches. Fellow Giant Jack Buckley is also out 4-6 weeks with a hamstring strain, joining Jesse Hogan (hip), Darcy Jones (knee), Xavier O'Halloran (ankle) and Cody Angove (hamstring) on the sidelines. The forward injury crisis leaves Toby Greene and Jake Stringer as the only forwards to play all 11 games, potentially boosting their scoring role and opportunity. The lone positive: All-Australian defender Sam Taylor returns this week from a high-grade hamstring strain, making him a watchlist POD despite being underdone after three months out.

news.headline: From larrikin to leader, veteran finds new purpose in Giants forward line
news.body: Jake Stringer has become a steady hand for GWS as he helps shape the club's young forwards.
news.ai_summary: Jake Stringer (GWS) is enjoying a durable, high-output season after a hamstring-plagued 2025, kicking 23 goals in 11 games — already nearing last year's 25 from 15 matches — including a five-goal haul against Brisbane. With Aaron Cadman, Jesse Hogan and Jake Riccardi all sidelined at various points, Stringer has been the only consistent forward, boosting his fantasy ceiling as the focal target. Cadman's return and the breakout of Phoenix Gothard are worth monitoring as forward options, with Gothard a potential cash-cow watch. Stringer himself admits his goalkicking accuracy has been inconsistent, suggesting further scoring upside in the second half of the year.

news.headline: Hawks to seek clarity from League on contentious Watson non-goal
news.body: Sam Mitchell says Hawthorn will speak to the AFL after Nick Watson's goal on the half-time siren was disallowed for running off his line.
news.ai_summary: Hawthorn's Jack Gunston was benched for the entire final quarter as a precaution with a lingering foot injury, making him a risky hold despite his two early goals. Will Day got through his managed minutes cleanly, while Conor Nash (neck spasm) was a late withdrawal but should return next week vs the Western Bulldogs. St Kilda's Sam Flanders appears headed for a lengthy stint after being helped off injured, but coaches can pencil in Nasiah Wanganeen-Milera, Mitch Owens and Liam Ryan (all calf) to return next week against Sydney. Nick Watson's disallowed half-time goal doesn't change his output, but Hawthorn forwards including Blake Hardwick and Jack Ginnivan cashed in during the 52-point win.

news.headline: Sluggish Saints left scrambling as Hawks cash in on blistering start
news.body: Hawthorn records a 52-point win after keeping St Kilda goalless in the first half.
news.ai_summary: Sam Flanders (St Kilda) looks set for an extended stint on the sidelines after an awkward landing did serious damage to his Achilles, a major hit for owners. Returning Hawthorn veteran Jack Gunston (5 goals) reasserted his forward value, leapfrogging Ben King atop the Coleman, while Blake Hardwick (4 goals) again cashed in pushed forward. Will Day returned via the bench for Hawthorn finishing with 18 disposals and 5 clearances, and Nick Watson chipped in 21 and 2. For the Saints, Jack Silvagni (3 goals, 10 marks) impressed swung forward, while Tom De Koning, Alix Tauru and Rowan Marshall offered late marking targets in the 119-67 loss.

news.headline: Dayne Zorko: Huge Lions blow with former skipper to miss six more weeks
news.body: Huge Lions blow with former skipper to miss six more weeks.
news.ai_summary: Brisbane veteran Dayne Zorko is set to miss another six weeks with a quad injury, ruling him out as a defensive option for fantasy sides considering a cheap value play. Coach Chris Fagan confirmed scans revealed further damage after the 37-year-old aggravated it at training. The Lions are also without Eric Hipwood, Jack Payne, Oscar Allen, Ryan Lester and Lincoln McCarthy, though Jarrod Berry and Noah Answerth return to face Fremantle. Avoid trading Zorko in, and his extended absence opens up potential role/scoring boosts for remaining Brisbane defenders.

news.headline: Blues reveal panel that will pick their next senior coach
news.body: West Coast premiership coach features among four-person panel to select Michael Voss' replacement.
news.ai_summary: No direct fantasy implications here, but Carlton's coaching search is worth monitoring for role changes in 2026. Adam Simpson will join Carlton CEO Graham Wright, president Rob Priestley and football boss Chris Davies on the four-person panel to replace Michael Voss, who departed after Carlton's 1-8 start. Interim coach Josh Fraser has steadied the ship with back-to-back wins, which could influence the structure and midfield/forward roles of key Blues fantasy assets like Patrick Cripps and Sam Walsh under a new regime.

news.headline: Saints face big test in Hawks clash at Marvel. LIVE from 7.30pm AEST

news.ai_summary: Hawthorn welcomes back two key fantasy-relevant names, with Will Day returning from foot/shoulder injuries (first AFL game since round 21 last year) and Jack Gunston back from a foot complaint — Bodie Ryan and Finn Maginness make way. St Kilda loses Dougal Howard (calf) and Liam Stocker (managed), recalling youngsters Alix Tauru and tall Isaac Keeler. Day is the headline hold/watch — expect a tagged role and ease-in minutes, while Gunston's return could dent the scoring of Hawks small forwards. Keeler's ruck/forward minutes are worth monitoring with Howard out.

news.headline: The man who could be the tallest player in VFL/AFL history
news.body: Richmond has landed 217cm giant Patrick Carr as a category B rookie.
news.ai_summary: Richmond has signed 217cm basketballer Patrick Carr as a Category B rookie, making him the tallest player ever listed in VFL/AFL history. Carr is a long-term ruck project with no senior football experience since his teens, so he holds no immediate fantasy relevance for SuperCoach or AFL Fantasy coaches. His arrival signals Richmond planning beyond Toby Nankervis, with Samson Ryan and Oliver Hayes-Brown still the ruckmen to monitor short-term.

news.body: Mitch Podhajski went from podiatrist to Magpie when Collingwood swooped on him in the Telstra AFL Mid-Season Rookie Draft.
news.ai_summary: Collingwood selected 27-year-old VFL forward Mitch Podhajski with pick 17 (second-last) in the Mid-Season Rookie Draft, giving Magpies coaches a mature-age forward option but minimal immediate fantasy relevance. The 191cm Coburg forward leads the VFL goalkicking with 27 goals from eight games (plus four in the Gather Round state game) after finishing second in the Frosty Miller Medal last year with 54 goals. Converted from defender to forward late in 2023 by coach Jamie Cassidy-McNamara, Podhajski is a deep rookie-list watch only — any AFL debut would likely come as a forward-line injury cover, making him a low-priority cash-cow prospect at best.

news.headline: The 25: FIVE Dockers make our list of the best 25 players in the AFL
news.body: Welcome back to 'The 25', where AFL.com.au's team of experts rank the best players in the game.
news.ai_summary:
**Fantasy Implications from The 25:**

Tom Bolton is in career-best form (24.4 disposals, 4.4 clearances, 13 goals) as a midfield/forward and remains a strong POD option. Josh Treacy's career-high averages (13.8 disposals, 7.2 marks, 2.3 goals) make him a premium forward hold. Jy Simpkin... err, Jai Newcombe is producing career-best numbers (25.5 disposals, 7.6 clearances, 4.1 tackles) and is a must-have mid. Brodie Grundy (35.8 hitouts) is the clear #1 ruck, while Matt Rowell owners should monitor closely given injury-affected form, and Lachie Neale (30.7 disposals) is back to elite output.

news.ai_summary: Essendon selected rebounding half-back Jaxon Artemis with pick No.1 in the Mid-Season Rookie Draft and, with 14 players (including Sam Durham via concussion) ruled out for the West Coast trip, the 19-year-old looms as an immediate rookie-cash option under caretaker coach Dean Solomon. Richmond grabbed defender Kye Annand at No.2 to cover Josh Gibcus, while West Coast took inside midfielder Oliver Francou (North Adelaide) at No.3 — a potential SuperCoach cash-cow given his clearance work. Ruck-needy coaches should note Carlton's Flynn Riley (No.4), Collingwood's Harrison Coe (No.8, covering Oscar Steene), Western Bulldogs' Caleb May (No.9) and Port Adelaide's Alex Van Wyk (No.14) all landing on lists.
